name: Vanilla / Direct-Prediction
descriptor: Architectures that don't impose equivariance as a hard constraint.
body: Direct-prediction models trade the equivariance guarantee for raw throughput. The headline example is the Orb family, which uses an equigrad training-time regularizer to encourage rotational consistency without enforcing it in the architecture. See Neumann et al., Orb and the v3 follow-up Rhodes et al., Orb-v3. What's gained is wall-clock speed (often 2–4× over equivariant siblings on the same hardware); what's risked is sporadic violations of physical consistency that can surface at long simulation timescales. Worth the tradeoff for many screening-scale workloads, less defensible for production MD.
